categories.title: ElizaOS Community Discussion and Project Updates

content.text: Community members discussed where to purchase ElizaOS tokens, with Shaw confirming that the most liquidity is currently on BSC. Shaw also noted that the ai16z to ElizaOS migration is no longer available, as the team that managed that process has since moved on. Shaw shared a video showcasing ElizaOS during this exchange.

A community member asked about the Babylon project after being unable to log in. Shaw explained that the project ran out of funding and was effectively shut down, with the team having moved on. Shaw noted that some elements of Babylon, specifically training simulations, have been repurposed and integrated into the new ElizaOS under the name Feed, though the full game was not carried over.

Other general community activity included a founder reaching out to learn how AI agent teams handle security when agents have wallet access, covering topics like spending caps and prompt injection. A senior engineer introduced themselves as a freelancer with experience in AI systems, Next.js, and RAG pipelines, expressing interest in connecting with other builders. A community member also asked about the roadmap for ElizaOS over the next few months, though no detailed response was captured in the discussion.

categories.title: Eliza Launches First Economic Agent Launchpad: Waifu

content.text: Eliza has launched its first economic agent launchpad called Waifu. The platform went live with its first agent named Sol, The Architect, who is described as the primary contributor to the Waifu platform. According to the discussion, Sol the Architect and an Eliza agent built the majority of this launchpad. The announcement was shared in the community discussion channel on May 24, 2026.

In the general discussion channel, two developers introduced themselves and their services. One full stack and AI developer highlighted experience in AI assistants, RAG-powered knowledge systems, workflow automation, SaaS platforms, and real-time applications, emphasizing secure and scalable production systems. A second AI/ML and full stack engineer outlined work in autonomous multi-agent systems, RAG pipelines, voice AI, workflow automation, and computer vision, with a tech stack spanning React, Next.js, Node.js, Python, PostgreSQL, AWS, and various LLM frameworks. A separate post advertised open roles for an onchain game project seeking testers, moderators, and Solidity developers on a remote and flexible basis. A technical issue was reported involving a file upload error on the Waifu platform, with the error message indicating missing operations and map fields during a flap upload attempt. A user also shared a post on social media calling out a crypto and AI commentator for being behind on key developments in the space. Additionally, a proposal was sent between community members regarding a previously discussed matter.

In the coders channel, a market update was shared covering Bitcoin, Solana, and XRP. Bitcoin was noted as consolidating around the 77,400 dollar range with strong dominance at a 1.45 trillion dollar market cap, with a notable headline involving Trump Media moving 2,650 BTC to Crypto.com. Solana was trading near 86.85 dollars, described as sensitive to macro sentiment but maintaining strong on-chain activity. XRP was hovering around 1.35 dollars with technical support at the 1.28 to 1.30 floor, driven by institutional accumulation and spot XRP ETF inflows, with traders watching for a push toward the 2.00 to 2.35 resistance range. The broader market tone was described as having deep liquidity anchored by stablecoins, with Tether at 184 billion and USDC at 79 billion, and whale rotation identified as the dominant theme of the week.

In the general discussion channel, a developer named Thomas Ken posted seeking collaboration opportunities or full-time hiring, sharing their portfolio link. Separately, a user named attic mel returned to the community after a break, asking about the status of the Eliza project. Odilitime, a core developer and moderator, clarified that the labs company is no longer operating but the open source project remains active. Attic mel also noted they had switched back to Linux and could not run JavaScript on their host, suggesting they might revisit the project in about four months. Attic mel additionally flagged a spam issue involving bots inviting users to fake support ticket rooms and requested that moderators develop countermeasures. A separate post from MDMnvest shared a link to an ElizaOS ecosystem fund announcement promoting a predictions game for ElizaOS token holders, described as offering utility for the community.
The coders channel had no messages or activity recorded for this date.

categories.title: Developer Introductions and AI Workflow Discussions

content.text: In the general discussion channel, two developers shared their backgrounds and recent work. Ginga introduced himself as an AI and full stack developer with experience in AI assistants, RAG-powered knowledge systems, workflow automation, SaaS platforms, and real-time applications. He emphasized building secure, scalable, and maintainable systems focused on practical business value rather than hype, and invited outreach from those building AI products or custom applications. Separately, waily announced the completion of a project called Resilience Bridge for an Australian client, described as a predictive platform designed to help people detect early financial or wellbeing stress and provide actionable plans. The project featured a predictive engine and an AI Coach component, took three months to complete, and is now in the handover phase. Waily expressed openness to new opportunities and offered assistance for similar projects.

In the coders channel, Xpes shared observations from experimenting with AgentKey MCP inside an OpenClaw setup while testing an AI workflow project. He noted that the integration allowed an AI agent to pull live Twitter and Reddit discussions for sentiment analysis and trend monitoring in real time, rather than relying on static summaries. The agent was able to compare real-time community narratives around topics and tokens, making outputs more dynamic. Xpes also explored autonomous research workflows and live search tasks using the tooling. He mentioned encountering some Windows-specific setup challenges but found the integration smoother than expected once configured. He noted that the MCP tooling space is becoming increasingly interesting and that testing of different workflows is ongoing.
In the general discussion channel, Odilitime clarified that Shaw has not launched any new token, addressing what appears to be community speculation or rumors on the topic.

In the coders channel, several notable posts appeared. Lefttoorz shared information about the Mantle Network Turing Test Hackathon, described as the first onchain benchmark for agentic AI at scale. The event features a $100K prize pool across six tracks including AI Trading and Strategy, AI Alpha and Data, AI and RWA, Consumer and Viral DApps, AI DevTools, and Agentic Wallets and Economy. Submissions are open from May 1 through June 15, 2026. Additionally, multiple users posted what appear to be solicitation or self-promotion messages, including a job listing for a project called Sunflower Polygon seeking beta testers, moderators, and developers, a freelance developer advertising full-stack and AI development services covering technologies such as Next.js, React, LangChain, and various blockchain integrations, and a small development team seeking a communication support person for international client work on a profit-based pay arrangement.

In the general discussion channel, several topics came up throughout the day. A representative from SKALE Labs named Chistormze reached out to Odilitime asking who the best person to contact regarding a partnership opportunity. A community member named Doc, identifying as a pre-snapshot holder from August 2025, asked about manual migration help after missing the February 4th portal deadline. Odilitime confirmed that manual migration is no longer available and that the migration process is closed. Separately, a user shared a detailed 0DTE options trade analysis covering key price levels including put concentration zones around 733 to 735, call wall resistance at 741, and upside targets at 745 to 747 and 750 to 755. The analysis described a spring-loaded setup with two trade strategies: a long bias above 741 targeting the call wall, and a defensive long around 734 to 735 if the put wall holds. The analysis cautioned against naked shorts below 730 and identified 735 to 740 as a no-trade zone with low risk-reward. The coders channel and partners channel had no activity recorded for this date.
